change_title: Emergency patch: Authentication bypass in OAuth flow
change_description: Emergency patch to fix an authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the OAuth2 authorization code flow. An attacker can craft a malicious redirect_uri that bypasses validation, allowing token theft. CVSS score: 9.1 (Critical). Patch replaces regex-based URI validation with strict allowlist matching.
business_justification: Critical security vulnerability actively being probed in the wild. WAF rules deployed as temporary mitigation but patch is required for full remediation. Regulatory obligation under SOC 2 Type II to patch critical vulnerabilities within 24 hours.
rollback_plan:
1. Revert auth-service to previous container image (sha:a3f8b21)
2. Re-enable WAF regex rules as interim protection
3. Verify OAuth flow with integration test suite
4. Estimated rollback: 5 minutes via container swap
test_evidence:
CI: https://ci.example.com/builds/auth-hotfix-9918
Security team pen-test: Vulnerability confirmed patched
OAuth integration tests: All 142 tests passing
Note: Staging soak test waived per emergency change policy

approvers.name: Raj Patel
approvers.rationale: Reviewed patch. Strict allowlist approach is correct. Emergency deploy approved.

approvers.name: Jennifer Walsh
approvers.rationale: Critical security fix. Emergency change policy invoked. CAB notified retroactively.
